name: GT-small-eta-threshold
source:
Kitaev arXiv:2405.02434 @ refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:525,532,2176. The eta<1/4 convergence threshold: Prop_P requires ||P^2-P||<=delta<1/4, its proof states X=2P-I satisfies ||X^2-I||<=4delta<1, and the applied tilde-Phi instance is a Taylor expansion in 4(Phi-Phi^2) which converges if eta<1/4. Under the rename P->Q, delta->eta, X=2P-I->S=2Q-I this is exactly ||S^2-I||=4||Q^2-Q||=4eta<1 for eta<1/4. VERBATIM: "Let $P$ be an element of a Banach algebra such that $\|P^2-P\|\le\delta<1/4$, and let
[...]
\begin{proof} The statement follow from the preceding discussion applied to $X=2P-I$, which satisfies the inequality $\|X^2-I\|\le 4\delta<1$.
[...]
The right-hand side involves a Taylor expansion in $4(\Phi-\Phi^2)$, which converges if $\eta<1/4$."
